seo.title: OpenAI Assistants API Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for OpenAI Assistants API agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams adopting OpenAI Assistants API face heightened risks due to the potential for hallucinated case citations, breaches of attorney-client privilege, and non-compliance with bar ethics rules. Without robust guardrails, assistants with code interpreters or file search can inadvertently expose confidential data or generate unverifiable legal content, leading to malpractice suits as seen in Mata v. Avianca. Vex provides verifiable compliance layers to ensure AI outputs meet court filing standards and eDiscovery protocols.

complianceRequirements.description: Client confidential information must remain protected and not be disclosed or used in AI processing without safeguards.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans Assistant outputs for PII/privileged data leaks and blocks transmission of sensitive threads.

complianceRequirements.regulation: Bar association ethics rules (e.g., ABA Model Rule 1.1)
complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal content must be reviewed by licensed attorneys and cannot replace competent representation.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags unverified AI outputs with watermarking and requires human attorney sign-off before use.

complianceRequirements.description: All case citations must reference real, verifiable precedents; fabricated citations risk sanctions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies citations against legal databases like Westlaw, rejecting hallucinated references.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-assisted document review must ensure completeness, accuracy, and no privilege waiver.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits file search results for relevance and privilege, preventing outdated or irrelevant retrievals.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Assistants invent non-existent cases, leading to court sanctions or case dismissal.
industryRisks.example: Mata v. Avianca (2023) where ChatGPT fabricated citations, resulting in judicial rebuke.

industryRisks.name: Privilege data exposure via code interpreter
industryRisks.description: Sandbox executes scripts that leak confidential client files.
industryRisks.example: Interpreter processes privileged PDFs and outputs excerpts in responses.

industryRisks.name: Thread context overflow
industryRisks.description: Growing conversation exceeds token limits, silently dropping critical legal context.
industryRisks.example: Long eDiscovery thread forgets initial privilege designations.

industryRisks.name: Function calling schema violations
industryRisks.description: Hallucinated arguments to legal APIs (e.g., PACER) cause invalid court queries.
industryRisks.example: Incorrect case numbers submitted to federal docket search.

implementation.title: Webhook for Run Completion Verification
implementation.description: Integrate Vex as a webhook on assistant run.completed events to scan for legal compliance issues.
implementation.code:
import openai
from vex_sdk import VexClient

client = openai.OpenAI()
vex = VexClient(api_key='vex-key')

def handle_run_completed(event):
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.retrieve(
        thread_id=event['data']['object']['thread_id'],
        run_id=event['data']['object']['id']
    )
    messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=run.thread_id)
    
    # Vex legal compliance check
    result = vex.verify({
        'prompt': 'Legal assistant output',
        'output': messages.data[0].content[0].text.value,
        'rules': ['no_hallucinated_citations', 'privilege_check', 'attorney_review_required']
    })
    if not result.passed:
        client.beta.threads.runs.submit_tool_outputs(
            thread_id=run.thread_id,
            run_id=run.id,
            tool_outputs=[{'tool_call_id': 'compliance_fail', 'output': result.issues}]
        )

implementation.title: TypeScript Webhook Handler
implementation.description: Serverless webhook using Node.js to verify Assistant outputs against legal schemas.

implementation.title: Python Thread Context Monitor
implementation.description: Proactively check thread token usage and trim to prevent silent context loss in legal threads.
implementation.code:
from openai import OpenAI
from vex_sdk import VexClient

client = OpenAI()
vex = VexClient(api_key='vex-key')

def monitor_legal_thread(thread_id: str):
    messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
    token_count = sum(len(msg.content[0].text.value) for msg in messages.data)
    
    if token_count > 100000:  # Near GPT-4 limit
        verification = vex.verify({
            'prompt': 'Legal thread context check',
            'context_length': token_count,
            'rules': ['thread_context_overflow']
        })
        if verification.warning:
            # Archive old messages
            for msg in messages.data[-10:]:
                client.beta.threads.messages.delete(thread_id, msg.id)
    
    # Legal-specific citation validation
    latest_content = messages.data[0].content[0].text.value
    citations = vex.extract_citations(latest_content)
    verified = vex.validate_legal_citations(citations)
    return verified

caseStudyScenario.heading: OpenAI Assistants API in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A mid-sized law firm deploys OpenAI Assistants for eDiscovery in a class-action lawsuit involving 10,000+ documents. The assistant uses file search to analyze privilege logs and code interpreter to generate summary reports. Junior associates query the assistant for case law on similar matters, feeding conversation history that grows to 200k+ tokens over weeks. The firm enables function calling to PACER for docket research. Without oversight, the assistant begins citing 'Smith v. Jones (2022, 2d Cir.)' – a fabricated case – in a motion draft. File search retrieves outdated privilege designations, risking waiver. Code interpreter processes a confidential client memo, inadvertently including excerpts in outputs sent to opposing counsel.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex webhook integration catches the hallucinated citation during run completion, cross-referencing against Westlaw and blocking submission. Thread monitoring flags context overflow at 90k tokens, automatically archiving non-essential messages while preserving privilege metadata. Privilege scanning redacts client data from interpreter outputs. Function call arguments are schema-validated before PACER submission. The motion is filed with verified citations only after attorney review confirmation via Vex watermark. The firm avoids sanctions, maintains privilege, and reduces review time by 40% with compliance assurance.
